title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: practice, initialize , add pack function intent time, run sync() whenever project match intent, save promote pack setup worth reusing.
code: flowchart TD   A([Start or clone project]) --> B(["Run init()"])   B --> C([Declare reusable intent])   C --> D(["Run add_pack()"])   C --> E(["Run add_function()"])   D --> F(["Run sync()"])   E --> F   F --> G[Hydrate from local libraries]   G --> H[Install remaining with pak]   H --> I[Snapshot declared packages]   F --> J[Helper files copied into boosters]   I --> K([Edit code and helper files])   J --> K   K --> L(["Run save_pack()"])   L --> M[Flat package pack or nested function pack]   M --> N(["Run promote_pack()"])   N --> O(["Reuse with add_pack() in another project"])   O --> D    classDef userAction fill:#e8f3ff,stroke:#1f6feb,color:#0b1f3a;   classDef packageAction fill:#f6f8fa,stroke:#6e7781,color:#24292f;   class A,B,C,D,E,F,K,L,N,O userAction;   class G,H,I,J,M packageAction;

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: creates: boosters.toml boosters/packs/ air.toml, enabled settings .Rprofile helper-source line, requested generated boosters.toml includes boosterpak [extras].declared, package needed run sync() also captured project intent project lockfile. renv = "yes", init() bootstraps renv, pak, boosterpak project library creates initial lockfile auto_snapshot = true. non-interactive sessions, use explicit renv rprofile arguments. default "ask" mode never silently changes .Rprofile user confirmation required.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Apply mode additive. hydrates plain-name missing packages renv-discoverable local libraries, installs anything still missing pak, materializes missing bundled installed helper functions, , auto_snapshot = true, snapshots renv. Use hydrate = FALSE skip local reuse stricter first-run installs.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: example pack installs cli. add_pack() preserves unrelated comments sections boosters.toml updating [packs].declared. analysis baseline fs, , janitor, rio, core tidyverse packages, scales, glue, digest, skimr, bundled helper functions, add eda pack explicitly: pack carries helper functions, add_pack() also copies bundled fn_*.R files boosters/ refuses overwrite existing function files unless overwrite_functions = TRUE. Packs can mix ordinary CRAN package names package-specific source specs. Declare every package packages, use [sources] packages install another source: , ggplot2 patchwork can hydrate install package name, ggtext skips hydration uses GitHub source spec.
code: boosterpak::add_pack("example") boosterpak::add_pack("eda") name = "plotting" description = "Plotting packages from CRAN and GitHub." packages = ["ggplot2", "patchwork", "ggtext"]  [sources] "ggtext" = "wilkelab/ggtext"

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Helper functions materialized boosters/fn_*.R files tracked [functions].installed. ordinary project files, can edit materialization. sync() restores missing installed helper files, overwrite local edits.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: save_pack() captures resolved package set , default, helper functions listed [functions].installed. Use functions = "" capture every boosters/fn_*.R file, functions = "none" flat package-pack, character vector specific function names. promote_pack() demote_pack() copy flat package-packs single files nested function-bearing packs whole directories.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: Restore mode requires boosters.toml renv.lock, calls renv::restore(), validates manifest, warns direct declared packages absent lockfile. remains lockfile-exact hydrate.

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: project initialized boosterpak 0.5 , restart, boosterpak longer found, run project without relying boosterpak loadable: restart R run:
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE) boosterpak::sync()

title: Getting started with boosterpak
text: status() reports whether manifest exists validates, packs packages resolve, whether project-local renv appears active, whether renv.lock exists, whether .Rprofile contains helper hook.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Sean Thimons. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Thimons S (2026). boosterpak: Declarative Project Package Bundles. R package version 0.5.0.9002, https://seanthimons.github.io/boosterpak/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {boosterpak: Declarative Project Package Bundles},   author = {Sean Thimons},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.5.0.9002},   url = {https://seanthimons.github.io/boosterpak/}, }

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: boosterpak R package declaring project package intent human-edited boosters.toml file. resolves named “booster” packs project, user, built-scopes, reuses locally available packages renv::hydrate() possible, installs anything still missing pak, uses renv project-local libraries lockfiles.

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: init() writes boosters.toml, creates boosters/packs/, optionally writes air.toml, manages .Rprofile helper-source line, can initialize project-local renv. renv = "yes", bootstraps renv, pak, boosterpak project library snapshots workflow packages restart needed.

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: usual loop initialize , add packs helper functions project intent, run sync(), capture useful baseline save_pack(). Additive installs hydrate plain-name packages local/user libraries downloading, install remaining packages pak. Package-packs stay flat boosters/packs/<name>.toml; packs carry copied helper files use boosters/packs/<name>/<name>.toml plus boosters/packs/<name>/functions/.
code: flowchart TD   A([Start or clone project]) --> B(["Run init()"])   B --> C([Declare reusable intent])   C --> D(["Run add_pack()"])   C --> E(["Run add_function()"])   D --> F(["Run sync()"])   E --> F   F --> G[Hydrate from local libraries]   G --> H[Install remaining with pak]   H --> I[Snapshot declared packages]   F --> J[Helper files copied into boosters]   I --> K([Edit code and helper files])   J --> K   K --> L(["Run save_pack()"])   L --> M[Flat package pack or nested function pack]   M --> N(["Run promote_pack()"])   N --> O(["Reuse with add_pack() in another project"])   O --> D    classDef userAction fill:#e8f3ff,stroke:#1f6feb,color:#0b1f3a;   classDef packageAction fill:#f6f8fa,stroke:#6e7781,color:#24292f;   class A,B,C,D,E,F,K,L,N,O userAction;   class G,H,I,J,M packageAction;

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: built-pack catalog contains: core: minimal bootstrap dependencies, pak renv. eda: analysis helpers including fs, , janitor, rio, core tidyverse packages, scales, glue, digest, skimr, bundled helper functions. example: small documented example installs cli. scaffold-analysis: installs fs carries helper compact analysis folder scaffold. github-example: installs ComptoxR seanthimons/ComptoxR. Packs can mix ordinary CRAN package names source-specific install specs. Declare every package packages, add [sources] entry packages come somewhere else: pack, ggplot2 patchwork install package name, ggtext uses GitHub source spec. Pack mutation additive. Removing pack updates boosters.toml can run sync, uninstall packages. default, add_pack() sync(mode = "apply") use hydrate = TRUE, ordinary CRAN-style package names can copied renv-discoverable local libraries pak downloads anything. Source-specific declarations, GitHub remotes [sources], skip hydration install declared spec. Use hydrate = FALSE stricter first-run installs go straight pak.
code: boosterpak::add_pack("example") name = "plotting" description = "Plotting packages from CRAN and GitHub." packages = ["ggplot2", "patchwork", "ggtext"]  [sources] "ggtext" = "wilkelab/ggtext"

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: save_pack() writes TOML snapshot currently resolved project packages , default, helper functions listed [functions].installed. Use functions = "" capture every boosters/fn_*.R file, functions = "none" flat package-pack, = "core" fork one existing pack, scope = "user" write directly machine-wide user pack directory. promote_pack() demote_pack() copy flat package-packs single files nested function-bearing packs whole directories.

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: sync(mode = "apply") treats boosters.toml intent renv.lock downstream output. may hydrate local libraries installing remaining declared packages pak. sync(mode = "restore") explicit path exact lockfile restoration hydrate.

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: project initialized boosterpak 0.5 restart made boosterpak unavailable inside renv, run without relying boosterpak loadable: restart R project run:
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE) boosterpak::sync()

title: Declarative Project Package Bundles
text: status() reports config validity, declared resolved packs, package counts, missing direct packages, function drift missing materialized files, pack catalog counts, renv state, lockfile presence, .Rprofile hook. Current development includes function materialization, pack capture/promotion, broader project status reporting; pruning remains scope.

Restore mode ignores option remains lockfile-exact. verbose Whether print routine summaries.

title: boosterpak 0.5.0.9001
text: sync(mode = "apply") eager add_pack() now hydrate plain-name missing packages renv-discoverable local libraries falling back pak::pkg_install(). Use hydrate = FALSE skip local reuse; sync(mode = "restore") remains exact lockfile restore path.

title: boosterpak 0.5.0.9000
text: init(renv = "yes") now initializes loads renv without immediate restart, installs bootstrap workflow packages pak, renv, boosterpak project library, snapshots auto_snapshot = true. built-core pack now minimal (pak, renv). previous analysis-oriented package set available new eda pack. Projects initialized boosterpak 0.5 can recover running: restart R run boosterpak::sync().
code: install.packages("renv") renv::install(c("pak", "seanthimons/boosterpak")) renv::snapshot(packages = c("renv", "pak", "boosterpak"), prompt = FALSE)
